compiled_state.title: [record-review.sh]: reviewer-findings.json from dso:code-reviewer-standard -> WARNING: review_tier missing or empty; cannot verify tier (fail-open)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

When \`dso:code-reviewer-standard\` (or any named code-reviewer agent) writes \`reviewer-findings.json\`, the \`review_tier\` field should be populated (e.g., \`"review_tier": "standard"\`). Downstream, \`record-review.sh\` reads this field to verify the review tier matches the classifier's selection, then records the review without emitting a warning.

#### Actual Behavior

During two successive review passes on the same commit (different diff hashes), \`record-review.sh\` emitted:

\`\`\`
WARNING: review_tier missing or empty in reviewer-findings.json — cannot verify tier; allowing review (fail-open)
Review status recorded: passed (score=4, diff_hash=48fca36ba66f...)
\`\`\`

And again on the second pass:

\`\`\`
WARNING: review_tier missing or empty in reviewer-findings.json — cannot verify tier; allowing review (fail-open)
Review status recorded: passed (score=5, diff_hash=077dc4daa4dc...)
\`\`\`

Both passes were dispatched as \`dso:code-reviewer-standard\` (classifier selected \`standard\` tier). The review itself recorded \`passed\`, but the missing \`review_tier\` field forced a fail-open path. This is silent data loss — record-review lost the information it uses to verify tier vs. classifier alignment.

### 3. Technical Environment

* Script: \`plugins/dso/hooks/record-review.sh\`
* Agent file: \`plugins/dso/agents/code-reviewer-standard.md\` (built by \`build-review-agents.sh\` — composed with a universal base fragment and a tier-specific delta)
* Review artifacts path: \`$ARTIFACTS_DIR/reviewer-findings.json\`

### 4. Action History

* Observation only (this session). The warning appears on every standard-tier review — not session-specific.

### 5. Investigation Pointers (non-speculative)

* \`plugins/dso/agents/code-reviewer-standard.md\` — confirm whether its output contract instructs the agent to emit \`review_tier\` in \`reviewer-findings.json\`
* The universal base fragment referenced in the agent header (\`<!-- content-hash: ... -->\` + \`<!-- generated by build-review-agents.sh -->\`) — check whether the shared JSON schema includes \`review_tier\` as a required field
* \`plugins/dso/scripts/build-review-agents.sh\` — check whether the tier name gets injected into the delta template when building the agent file
* \`plugins/dso/hooks/record-review.sh\` — confirm the warning emits unconditionally when the field is absent, and consider whether it should be an ERROR (not a WARNING) if reviewer-findings.json is required to carry tier metadata
